architecture.summary: Persistent Visual Memory (PVM) is a lightweight learnable module integrated as a parallel branch alongside the Feed-Forward Network (FFN) in Large Vision-Language Models. It establishes a distance-agnostic retrieval pathway that directly provides visual embeddings during autoregressive generation, mitigating visual signal dilution caused by textual history expansion and attention partition decay.

biblio.title: Persistent Visual Memory: Sustaining Perception for Deep Generation in LVLMs

claims.statement: PVM brings notable improvements with negligible parameter overhead, delivering consistent average accuracy gains across both 4B and 8B scales, particularly in complex reasoning tasks that demand persistent visual perception.
PVM shows improved robustness in longer generations and accelerates internal prediction convergence.
Visual Signal Dilution phenomenon: the accumulation of textual history expands the attention partition function, causing visual attention to decay inversely with generated sequence length.
